title: Secondary water resistance: when roof work is really needed
description: Use this route when SWR creates the exception question and you need to know whether broader roof work is really required.
recommendationMeaning: SWR recommendations usually mean the roof assembly needs a mitigation upgrade that can affect whether broader roof work is part of the project path. The right move is to understand the causal link before budgeting around replacement.
rightFirstProjectSignals: The report clearly points to SWR.
The proposed roof work is being justified as necessary to complete the eligible mitigation recommendation.
You need to know whether the quote is really about mitigation or just about a general roof sale.
qualifyingScope: SWR-related work should stay tied to the eligible recommendation.
If full roof work is proposed, the quote should explain why that scope is necessary.
You should separate eligible mitigation work from unrelated roof wish-list items.
attachedHomeCaveat: Attached-home owners should stop and verify scope before following a roof-heavy sales path.
confusionOrDenialRisks: Treating SWR as a blanket approval for a reroof.
Assuming reimbursement will cover every roofing line item attached to an eligible SWR job.

secondaryCta.label: Check whether SWR really justifies replacement
